facade_colour: warm red-brown brick with dark trim and decorative brick detailing

photo_observations.facade_colour_observed: warm red-brown brick with dark brown trim, decorative brick banding and arched details
photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: Good condition. Well-preserved Victorian brick facade with rich decorative brickwork. Some paint/modification at ground floor entries but upper floors intact.
photo_observations.window_details: Ground floor has two large rounded arch openings with brick voussoirs and decorative archivolts. Second floor has three windows with segmental arched brick heads. Gable has single window. Decorative brick courses frame the window openings.
photo_observations.door_details: Two entries at ground level within rounded arch openings, suggesting semi-detached pair. Blue/dark painted doors visible.
photo_observations.cornice_details: Decorative brick corbelling at eave line with bracketed detail
photo_observations.porch_details: Low stoops at both entries with steps
photo_observations.decorative_details: Exceptionally rich decorative brickwork. Large rounded arches at ground floor with prominent brick voussoirs/archivolts. Continuous decorative string course between floors. Segmental arched window heads at second floor. Steep gable peaks with decorative trim. One of the most ornate facades on Nassau St.
photo_observations.roof_type_observed: Twin steep gables, bay-and-gable form for semi-detached pair
photo_observations.alterations_visible: Ground floor entry treatments may be modified but arched openings are original. Upper floors well-preserved.
photo_observations.notes: Striking 2.5-storey semi-detached Victorian pair in warm red-brown brick. Prominent rounded arched openings at ground floor with elaborate brick voussoirs. Three windows at second floor with segmental arches. Twin steep gable peaks. Rich decorative brickwork throughout. One of the best-preserved Victorian facades on Nassau St.

notes: Striking 2.5-storey Victorian semi-detached pair. Red-brown brick with prominent rounded arches at ground floor. Rich decorative brickwork. Twin steep gables.

facade_detail.composition: The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 3 storeys and is capped by a gable, consistent with its house-form typology.
facade_detail.opening_rhythm: The opening rhythm is vertically ordered across the facade, using windows, and 1 principal entrance.

facade_description: Three-storey house-form (c. 1889-1903) in warm red-brown brick with decorative brick detailing throughout. Twin steep gables suggest a semi-detached pair. Ground floor features two prominent rounded arch openings with brick voussoirs; second floor has three windows with segmental arched heads and a continuous string course. Decorative corbelling at the eave line. One of the most ornate facades on Nassau Street.
